name: Irish Soda Bread
recipeIngredient: 4 cups all-purpose flour or whole wheat flour
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, baking powder, salt, and sugar. Cut in cold butter with a pastry blender or two knives until the mixture is fine.
2. Stir in raisins.
3. In another bowl, beat eggs and stir in buttermilk and soda. Make a well in the dry ingredients and add liquids all at once, mixing lightly with a fork until blended.
4. Turn dough out onto a floured surface and knead lightly for 10 seconds. Shape dough into a ball and place on a buttered baking sheet.
5. Flatten the ball into an 8 or 9-inch loaf.
6. Using a sharp knife greased with butter, cut 1/4-inch deep slits from edge to edge, marking into quarters.
7. Brush the top with melted butter; bake in a preheated 375°F oven for 40 to 45 minutes, or until golden brown. Remove from the baking sheet and cool to lukewarm.
